qa_pairs.question: What is the Trillium cluster, and when will it be available for use?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium is a large parallel cluster built by Lenovo Canada and hosted by SciNet at the University of Toronto, with an availability date of August 7, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key specifications for the CPU and GPU login nodes available on the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPU login node, trillium.alliancecan.ca, has 1224 nodes, 192 cores, 749GB of available memory, and 2 x AMD EPYC 9655 (Zen 5) @ 2.6 GHz CPUs. The GPU login node, trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca, has 63 nodes, 96 cores, 749GB of available memory, 1 x AMD EPYC 9654 (Zen 4) @ 2.4 GHz CPU, and 4 x NVidia H100 SXM (80 GB memory) GPUs connected via NVLink.

qa_pairs.question: What are the storage and high-performance network characteristics of the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium has 29 petabytes of parallel NVMe SSD-based storage from VAST Data, with 29 PB effective capacity and an additional 114 PB HPSS tape-based archive. Its high-performance network uses Nvidia "NDR" Infiniband, providing 400 Gbit/s bandwidth for CPU nodes and 800 Gbit/s for GPU nodes, and is fully non-blocking.
